member.name: T:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.DocumentFileInfo
member.summary: Represents a source file that is part of a project file.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.DocumentFileInfo.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{System.String})
member.summary: Represents a source file that is part of a project file.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.DocumentFileInfo.FilePath
member.summary: The absolute path to the document file on disk.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.DocumentFileInfo.LogicalPath
member.summary: A fictional path to the document, relative to the project. The document may not actually exist at this location, and is used to represent linked documents. This includes the file name.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.DocumentFileInfo.IsLinked
member.summary: True if the document has a logical path that differs from its absolute file path.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.DocumentFileInfo.IsGenerated
member.summary: True if the file was generated during build.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.DocumentFileInfo.Folders
member.summary: Containing folders of the document relative to the containing project root path.

member.name: T:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.IBuildHost
member.summary: RPC methods.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.IBuildHost.FindBestMSBuild(System.String)
member.summary: Finds the best MSBuild instance installed for loading the given project or solution.
member.remarks: This may return MSBuild instances that are not loadable by the BuildHost process.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.IBuildHost.HasUsableMSBuild(System.String)
member.summary: Determines whether there is a MSBuild instance that is loadable by the BuildHost process.
member.remarks: This may return true even if the project or solution require a newer version of MSBuild.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.IBuildHost.ConfigureGlobalState(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableDictionary{System.String,System.String},System.String)
member.summary: Called once on a new process to configure some global state. This is used for these rather than passing through command line strings, since these contain data that might contain paths (which can have escaping issues) or could be quite large (which could run into length limits).

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.IBuildHost.LoadProject(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Permits loading a project file which only exists in-memory, for example, for file-based program scenarios.
member.param: A path to a project file which may or may not exist on disk. Note that an extension that is known by MSBuild, such as .csproj or .vbproj, should be used here.

member.name: T:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.IProjectFile
member.summary: RPC methods.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.MonoMSBuildDiscovery.RealPath(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the canonicalized absolute path from a given path, expanding symbolic links and resolving references to /./, /../ and extra '/' path characters.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.MonoMSBuildDiscovery.GetMonoRuntimeExecutablePath
member.summary: Returns the fully qualified path to the mono executable.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.MonoMSBuildDiscovery.GetMonoLibDirPath
member.summary: Returns the path to the mono lib directory, usually /usr/bin/mono.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.MonoMSBuildDiscovery.GetMonoMSBuildDirectory
member.summary: Returns the path to MSBuild, the actual directory containing MSBuild.dll and friends. Usually should end in Current/bin.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.MSBuildLocation.Path
member.summary: This is the path to the directory containing the MSBuild binaries.
member.remarks: When running on .NET this will be the path to the SDK required for loading projects.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.MSBuildLocation.Version
member.summary: This is the version of MSBuild at this location.

member.name: T:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo
member.summary: Provides information about a project that has been loaded from disk and built with MSBuild. If the project is multi-targeting, this represents the information from a single target framework.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.Language
member.summary: The language of this project.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.FilePath
member.summary: The path to the project file for this project.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.OutputFilePath
member.summary: The path to the output file this project generates.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.OutputRefFilePath
member.summary: The path to the reference assembly output file this project generates.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.IntermediateOutputFilePath
member.summary: The path to the intermediate output file this project generates.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.DefaultNamespace
member.summary: The default namespace of the project ("" if not defined, which means global namespace), or null if it is unknown or not applicable.
member.remarks: Right now VB doesn't have the concept of "default namespace". But we conjure one in workspace by assigning the value of the project's root namespace to it. So various feature can choose to use it for their own purpose. In the future, we might consider officially exposing "default namespace" for VB project (e.g. through a "defaultnamespace" msbuild property)

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.TargetFramework
member.summary: The target framework of this project. This takes the form of the 'short name' form used by NuGet (e.g. net46, netcoreapp2.0, etc.)

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.TargetFrameworkIdentifier
member.summary: The target framework identifier of this project. Used to determine if a project is targeting .net core.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.CommandLineArgs
member.summary: The command line args used to compile the project.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.Documents
member.summary: The source documents.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.AdditionalDocuments
member.summary: The additional documents.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.AnalyzerConfigDocuments
member.summary: The analyzer config documents.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.ProjectReferences
member.summary: References to other projects.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.ProjectCapabilities
member.summary: The msbuild project capabilities.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.ContentFilePaths
member.summary: The paths to content files included in the project.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.ProjectAssetsFilePath
member.summary: The path to the project.assets.json path in obj/.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.PackageReferences
member.summary: Any package references defined on the project.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileInfo.TargetFrameworkVersion
member.summary: Target framework version (for .net framework projects)

member.name: T:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileReference
member.summary: Represents a reference to another project file.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileReference.Path
member.summary: The path on disk to the other project file. This path may be relative to the referencing project's file or an absolute path.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileReference.Aliases
member.summary: The aliases assigned to this reference, if any.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.ProjectFileReference.ReferenceOutputAssembly
member.summary: The value of "ReferenceOutputAssembly" metadata.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.Request.Id
member.summary: The request ID.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.Request.TargetObject
member.summary: The index of the targeted object.

member.name: P:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.Request.Method
member.summary: The name of the method to call.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.MSBuild.TextReaderExtensions.TryReadLineOrReturnNullIfCancelledAsync(System.IO.TextReader,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the next line from the string, or null if the stream is closed or the cancellation token was triggered.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfNull``1(``0,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws a non-accessible exception if the provided value is null. This method executes in all builds

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfNull``1(System.Nullable{``0},System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws a non-accessible exception if the provided value is null. This method executes in all builds

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfNull``1(``0,System.String,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws a non-accessible exception if the provided value is null. This method executes in all builds

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfNull``1(``0,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfNullInterpolatedStringHandler{``0},System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws a non-accessible exception if the provided value is null. This method executes in all builds

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfFalse(System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws a non-accessible exception if the provided value is false. This method executes in all builds

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfFalse(System.Boolean,System.String,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws a non-accessible exception if the provided value is false. This method executes in all builds

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfFalse(System.Boolean,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfFalseInterpolatedStringHandler,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws a non-accessible exception if the provided value is false. This method executes in all builds

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfTrue(System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws a non-accessible exception if the provided value is true. This method executes in all builds.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfTrue(System.Boolean,System.String,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws a non-accessible exception if the provided value is true. This method executes in all builds.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfTrue(System.Boolean,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Contract.ThrowIfTrueInterpolatedStringHandler,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws a non-accessible exception if the provided value is true. This method executes in all builds.

member.name: F:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FailFast.Handler
member.summary: A pre-created delegate to assign to if needed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FailFast.DumpStackTrace(System.Exception,System.String)
member.summary: Dumps the stack trace of the exception and the handler to the console. This is useful for debugging unit tests that hit a fatal exception

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FailFast.Assert(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Checks for the given ; if the is true, immediately terminates the process without running any pending finally blocks or finalizers and causes a crash dump to be collected (if the system is configured to do so). Otherwise, the process continues normally.
member.param: An optional message to be recorded in the dump in case of failure. Can be null.

member.name: T:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.OperationCanceledIgnoringCallerTokenException
member.summary: Thrown when async code must cancel the current execution but does not have access to the of the passed to the code. Should be used in very rare cases where the is out of our control (e.g. owned but not exposed by JSON RPC in certain call-back scenarios).

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.SetHandlers(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.ErrorReporterHandler,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.ErrorReporterHandler)
member.summary: Set by the host to handle an error report; this may crash the process or report telemetry.
member.param: A handler that will not crash the process when called. Used when calling

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.OverwriteHandler(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.ErrorReporterHandler)
member.summary: Same as setting the Handler property except that it avoids the assert. This is useful in test code which needs to verify the handler is called in specific cases and will continually overwrite this value.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.CopyHandlersTo(System.Reflection.Assembly)
member.summary: Copies the handler in this instance to the linked copy of this type in this other assembly.
member.remarks: This file is in linked into multiple layers, but we want to ensure that all layers have the same copy. This lets us copy the handler in this instance into the same in another instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.ReportAndPropagate(System.Exception,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity)
member.summary: Use in an exception filter to report an error without catching the exception. The error is reported by calling .

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.ReportAndPropagateUnlessCanceled(System.Exception,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity)
member.summary: Use in an exception filter to report an error (by calling ), unless the operation has been cancelled. The exception is never caught.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.ReportAndPropagateUnlessCanceled(System.Exception,System.Threading.CancellationToken,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity)

summary.para: Use in an exception filter to report an error (by calling ), unless the operation has been cancelled at the request of . The exception is never caught.

Cancellable operations are only expected to throw if the applicable indicates cancellation is requested by setting . Unexpected cancellation, i.e. an which occurs without requesting cancellation, is treated as an error by this method.
This method does not require to match , provided cancellation is expected per the previous paragraph.

member.param: A which will have set if cancellation is expected.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.ReportAndCatch(System.Exception,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity)
member.summary: Report an error. Calls and doesn't pass the exception through (the method returns true). This is generally expected to be used within an exception filter as that allows us to capture data at the point the exception is thrown rather than when it is handled. However, it can also be used outside of an exception filter. If the exception has not already been thrown the method will throw and catch it itself to ensure we get a useful stack trace.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.ReportAndCatchUnlessCanceled(System.Exception,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity)
member.summary: Use in an exception filter to report an error (by calling ) and catch the exception, unless the operation was cancelled.
member.returns: to catch the exception if the error was reported; otherwise, to propagate the exception if the operation was cancelled.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.ReportAndCatchUnlessCanceled(System.Exception,System.Threading.CancellationToken,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity)

summary.para: Use in an exception filter to report an error (by calling ) and catch the exception, unless the operation was cancelled at the request of .

Cancellable operations are only expected to throw if the applicable indicates cancellation is requested by setting . Unexpected cancellation, i.e. an which occurs without requesting cancellation, is treated as an error by this method.
This method does not require to match , provided cancellation is expected per the previous paragraph.

member.param: A which will have set if cancellation is expected.
member.returns: to catch the exception if the error was reported; otherwise, to propagate the exception if the operation was cancelled.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.FatalError.ReportNonFatalError(System.Exception,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Used to report a non-fatal-watson (when possible) to report an exception. The exception is not caught. Does nothing if no non-fatal error handler is registered. See the second argument to .

member.name: T:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity
member.summary: The severity of the error, see the enum members for a description of when to use each. This is metadata that's included in a non-fatal fault report, which we can take advantage of on the backend to automatically triage bugs. For example, a critical severity issue we can open with a lower bug count compared to a low priority one.

member.name: F:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity.Uncategorized
member.summary: The severity hasn't been categorized. Don't use this in new code.

member.name: F:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity.Diagnostic
member.summary: Something failed, but the user is unlikely to notice. Especially useful for background things that we can silently recover from, like bugs in caching systems.

member.name: F:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity.General
member.summary: Something failed, and the user might notice, but they're still likely able to carry on. For example, if the user asked for some information from the IDE (find references, completion, etc.) and we were able to give partial results.

member.name: F:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ErrorReporting.ErrorSeverity.Critical
member.summary: Something failed, and the user likely noticed. For example, the user pressed a button to do an action, and we threw an exception so we completely failed to do that in an unrecoverable way. This may also be used for back-end systems where a failure is going to result in a highly broken experience, for example if parsing a file catastrophically failed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ExceptionUtilities.UnexpectedValue(System.Object)
member.summary: Creates an with information about an unexpected value.
member.returns: The , which should be thrown by the caller.

member.name: M:Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ExceptionUtilities.IsCurrentOperationBeingCancelled(System.Exception,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Determine if an exception was an , and that the provided token caused the cancellation.
member.param: Checked to see if the provided token was cancelled.
member.returns: if the exception was an and the token was canceled.
